name: The Eagle
description: Historic pub where Watson and Crick discussed the structure of DNA. Cosy panelled rooms and a famous RAF bar.

name: The Mill
description: Riverside pub by the Cam with a large terrace. Popular with students and punters.

name: The Pickerel Inn
description: One of Cambridge's oldest pubs. Cosy fireside seating and a relaxed atmosphere.

name: Fort St George
description: Riverside pub on Midsummer Common with great views of the Cam and punting.

name: The Anchor
description: Waterfront pub on Silver Street with a large terrace. Perfect for a pint by the river.

name: The Alexandra Arms
description: CAMRA Good Beer Guide regular. Real ale focus and a friendly local vibe.

name: Elm Tree
description: Tiny backstreet pub with an impressive rotating selection of real ales and ciders.

name: Baron of Beef
description: Traditional pub near the river with a good range of ales and pub food.

name: The Cambridge Blue
description: Multi-room pub with a huge beer garden. Extensive ale and cider list.

name: The Free Press
description: Small, no-nonsense pub with a loyal following. No music, no fruit machines.

name: The Granta
description: Riverside pub with a terrace overlooking the mill pond. Popular for summer drinks.

name: The Haymakers
description: Community pub in Chesterton with real ales and a welcoming atmosphere.

name: The Mitre
description: Historic pub on Bridge Street with wood-panelled interior and a solid ale selection.

name: The Panton Arms
description: Corner pub near the market. Good for a quick pint and people-watching.

name: The Pint Shop
description: Craft beer and grill house in a central location. Wide selection of keg and cask.

name: The Royal Standard
description: Unpretentious local on Mill Road with a mixed crowd and regular events.

name: The Blue Moon
description: Small pub with a focus on real ale and live music. LGBTQ+ friendly.

name: BrewBoard Taproom
description: Taproom for local BrewBoard brewery. Fresh cask and keg in the heart of town.
